title: Deliciously Spicy French Cheese Terrine
description: A spicy and gooey French dish featuring semolina flour, perfect for a kid-friendly meal, this Basque-French terrine is a satvik-friendly delight.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, semolina flour, and a pinch of salt, and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the shredded gruyere cheese until melted and smooth.
Add the butter, paprika, cayenne pepper, and black pepper to the cheese mixture and stir until well combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk the eggs and season with a pinch of salt.
Add the egg mixture to the cheese mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a 9x5 inch terrine mold or loaf pan.
Bake the terrine in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until set and golden brown on top.
Remove the terrine from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Slice the terrine and serve warm or at room temperature.
